answer: To access the Plaid API, you must provide your client_id and secret, which are obtained from the Plaid Dashboard [1]. These credentials are required for almost all API endpoints and can be included either as headers (PLAID-CLIENT-ID and PLAID-SECRET) or within the request body [1][2]. When using Plaid’s official server-side client libraries, you can initialize the configuration once to automatically include these credentials in all subsequent requests [3]. Alternatively, you can use the Plaid Postman collection to simplify the process of formatting these requests [1].
